name: Crab Apple Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the crab apples thoroughly, removing any stems or damaged areas.
2. Place the crab apples and water in a large pot and cook until the apples are soft.
3. Strain the mixture through a jelly bag to obtain approximately 7 cups of juice.
4. In a large kettle, combine the strained juice and powdered pectin.
5. Bring the mixture to a full boil over high heat, stirring constantly.
6. Gradually stir in the sugar until it is completely dissolved.
7. Continue boiling for an additional minute while stirring.
8. Remove from heat and skim any foam that forms on the surface.
9. Pour the hot jelly into sterilized jars, wiping away any drips from both the inside and outside of the jars.
10. Seal the jars with hot lids and secure with screw bands.
11. Process the sealed jars in a boiling water bath for 10 minutes.
Makes: 10 cups.
